verses.text: Belshazzar feasts and drinks wine from the temple vessels, praising gods of gold and silver. Sacrilege in revelry. He defies the true God at the height of his pleasure.
Belshazzar feasts and drinks wine from the temple vessels, praising gods of gold and silver. Sacrilege in revelry. He defies the true God at the height of his pleasure.
Belshazzar feasts and drinks wine from the temple vessels, praising gods of gold and silver. Sacrilege in revelry. He defies the true God at the height of his pleasure.
Belshazzar feasts and drinks wine from the temple vessels, praising gods of gold and silver. Sacrilege in revelry. He defies the true God at the height of his pleasure.
A man's hand writes on the wall; the terrified king calls the wise men to read it, offering rewards. Sudden dread. One sign from God shatters all his confidence.
A man's hand writes on the wall; the terrified king calls the wise men to read it, offering rewards. Sudden dread. One sign from God shatters all his confidence.
A man's hand writes on the wall; the terrified king calls the wise men to read it, offering rewards. Sudden dread. One sign from God shatters all his confidence.
They cannot read nor interpret it, and the king is greatly troubled. Wisdom fails. The learning of Babylon is dumb before the writing of God.
They cannot read nor interpret it, and the king is greatly troubled. Wisdom fails. The learning of Babylon is dumb before the writing of God.
The queen tells him of Daniel, in whom is 'the spirit of the holy gods,' able to interpret. A timely word. God always has His witness when He is needed.
The queen tells him of Daniel, in whom is 'the spirit of the holy gods,' able to interpret. A timely word. God always has His witness when He is needed.
The queen tells him of Daniel, in whom is 'the spirit of the holy gods,' able to interpret. A timely word. God always has His witness when He is needed.
Daniel is brought, and the king offers him gifts to read the writing. Bribes proffered. The world thinks God's truth can be bought.

Daniel is brought, and the king offers him gifts to read the writing. Bribes proffered. The world thinks God's truth can be bought.
Daniel refuses the gifts and recalls how Nebuchadnezzar was abased for pride till he knew the most High rules. History recalled. The lesson Belshazzar should have learned was before him.

Daniel refuses the gifts and recalls how Nebuchadnezzar was abased for pride till he knew the most High rules. History recalled. The lesson Belshazzar should have learned was before him.
'But thou... hast not humbled thine heart,' lifting thyself against heaven and drinking from His vessels; therefore was the hand sent. Pride unrepented. To sin against known truth seals the doom.
'But thou... hast not humbled thine heart,' lifting thyself against heaven and drinking from His vessels; therefore was the hand sent. Pride unrepented. To sin against known truth seals the doom.
'But thou... hast not humbled thine heart,' lifting thyself against heaven and drinking from His vessels; therefore was the hand sent. Pride unrepented. To sin against known truth seals the doom.

The writing is read: MENE, TEKEL, UPHARSIN — 'God hath numbered thy kingdom... thou art weighed... and found wanting'; it is given to the Medes and Persians. The verdict. Every life is weighed, and the proud are found wanting.

The writing is read: MENE, TEKEL, UPHARSIN — 'God hath numbered thy kingdom... thou art weighed... and found wanting'; it is given to the Medes and Persians. The verdict. Every life is weighed, and the proud are found wanting.
Daniel is honoured, but that very night Belshazzar is slain, and Darius takes the kingdom. Swift judgment. The sentence of God fell the same hour it was read.
